hypothesis: Numerical claims drift at ~5-20% unsupported rate after 50 sessions
method: 20-claim sample biased toward numerical claims (16 numerical, 4 existence). Sourced from FRONTIER.md, INDEX.md, DEPS.md, NEXT.md, PRINCIPLES.md, PHILOSOPHY.md. Each claim verified against filesystem/tool evidence.

claims.actual: 16 frontier bullet entries counted
claims.notes: Exact match

claims.actual: 43 domain FRONTIER.md files found
claims.notes: 43 vs 36 = 19.4% drift. 7 new domain frontiers added since this was written. Stale by an indeterminate number of sessions.

claims.actual: Current session is S405+ (this is S405). INDEX.md says 404.
claims.notes: Off by 1 session — typical lag from concurrent sessions. Within 1%.

claims.actual: 793 L-*.md files in lessons/ directory. 794 total files including TEMPLATE.md.
claims.notes: Off by 1-2 lessons. Concurrent session may have added lessons after INDEX.md update.

claims.actual: 207 unique P-IDs in PRINCIPLES.md. 14 are inline '(P-NNN merged)' references. contract_check.py confirms P=196. Net count ~193 by direct count but tool says 196.
claims.notes: Within counting-method variance. The authoritative tool (contract_check.py) confirms 196.

claims.actual: 20 belief headers (### B*) found
claims.notes: Exact match

claims.actual: 16 active frontier entries in FRONTIER.md
claims.notes: 21 vs 16. NEXT.md S404d explicitly documents '21→16 active frontiers' from TTL triage. INDEX.md was not updated to reflect this. 5 frontiers were removed (3 ABANDONED + 1 RESOLVED + 1 MERGED per FRONTIER.md header).

claims.actual: 43 domain directories. 43 domain FRONTIER.md files.
claims.notes: 30 vs 43. The domain count has grown significantly. 43% drift.

claims.claim: Themes sum to 792 lessons across 33 themes
claims.actual: Theme counts sum to 736. 33 theme rows present. 793 actual lesson files.
claims.notes: Theme counts sum to 736 but INDEX header says 792 lessons and there are 793 files. 57 lessons are unaccounted for in the theme table (7.2% gap). Theme allocation lags lesson creation.

claims.actual: 19 observed, 1 theorized. Only B14 remains theorized. B19 changed to observed (PARTIALLY FALSIFIED), B15 changed to observed (S397).
claims.notes: Evidence levels changed for 3 beliefs since this header was written. 16→19 observed, 4→1 theorized. The header was not updated when individual belief evidence levels changed.

claims.claim: 152 frontiers, 88% resolution, 36/36 domains generating via UCB1
claims.actual: 16 active + 140 archived = 156 total frontiers. 140/156 = 89.7% resolution. 43 domain frontier files (not 36).
claims.notes: Total frontiers close (152 vs 156 = 2.6% off). Resolution rate close (88% vs 89.7%). Domain count stale (36 vs 43 = 19.4% off). Mixed: frontier/resolution PLAUSIBLE, domain count CONTRADICTED.

claims.actual: At S356, there were ~356 sessions, not 404. The '404' appears to be a later update of the count while retaining the 'S356' provenance tag.
claims.notes: The current session count (~405) is close to 404. The number was likely updated recently but the source tag was not. The claim is approximately correct for current state but misattributed to S356.

claims.actual: Meta-themed lessons in INDEX.md sum to 342 across 12 meta sub-themes. At S354, corpus was ~580L so 96/580=16.6% (not 18.5%). Currently 342/793=43.1%.
claims.notes: The '96 lessons' was a S354-era measurement. Meta lessons are now 342 (3.6x growth). The '18.5%' was wrong even then (should have been ~16.6%). The frontier text is a historical snapshot, not a current claim, but the numbers are presented without temporal context.

claims.actual: Currently ~405 sessions. Still 0 external outputs (this is likely still true).
claims.notes: Session count stale (389 vs ~405 = 4.1% drift). The '0 external outputs' claim is likely still correct — no evidence of any external output being produced. Written at S389.

claims.actual: 1 DROPPED challenge (PHIL-7 DROPPED at S389). F-META15 was written at S393 but cites '388 sessions'.
claims.notes: The claim says 'in 388 sessions' and PHIL-7 was DROPPED at S389 (session 389). So the claim is literally correct for its stated scope (first 388 sessions). The data point is stale but not wrong.

claims.actual: contract_check.py confirms 196 principles. But there are only 7 theme headers (##) in PRINCIPLES.md: Architecture, Protocols, Strategy, Complexity, Evolution, Governance, Distributed Systems.
claims.notes: Principle count confirmed (196). Theme count off by 1 (8 claimed vs 7 found). Possible that 'Governance' was split at some point or an 8th theme was removed.

claims.actual: INDEX.md NK Structure theme says 'K_avg=2.56 CROSSED'. MEMORY.md says 'K_avg=2.09' (S359). B9 says 2.587 (S396). Multiple different K_avg values across files.
claims.notes: Three different K_avg values in three files: 2.09 (MEMORY.md, S359), 2.56 (INDEX.md), 2.587 (DEPS.md, S396). No way to determine which is current without running nk_null_model.py. The values are from different sessions and measurement methods. INDEX.md and B9 are approximately consistent (2.56 vs 2.587); MEMORY.md is from an older era.

claims.actual: 43 domain directories exist. Session context was S356 (355 sessions). At current state (S405), still 43 directories.
claims.notes: Close enough for the era when written. 45 vs 43 is within counting-method variance (some 'domains' in experiments/ may have been counted). The session count (355) is correctly scoped to S356.

claims.actual: 33 items in experiments/children/ directory confirmed
claims.notes: Exact match

claims.actual: At S355, this was correct. Now there is 1 DROPPED (PHIL-7 at S389). The claim is scoped to S355 era and was accurate then.
claims.notes: Historically accurate. The landscape has changed (1 DROPPED now exists) but the claim is properly scoped.

analysis.trend: Worst result in F-QC5 history. Prior S387 numerical-biased sample was 20%; this is 35%. The gap has widened, not narrowed.
root_causes: COUNT DRIFT: 4 of 6 contradictions are stale counts (frontiers 21→16, domains 30→43 and 36→43, themes summing to 736 not 792, observed/theorized split 16/4→19/1). Counts are written once and never updated when the underlying data changes.
CROSS-FILE INCONSISTENCY: Same metric (K_avg, domain count, frontier count) has different values in different files because each was updated at a different time.
SNAPSHOT WITHOUT TIMESTAMP: F-META8's '96-lesson mass' was a S354 measurement but is presented as if current. No temporal markers distinguish historical measurements from current claims.

HEADER LAG: File headers (INDEX.md, DEPS.md interconnection model) are updated less frequently than the body content they summarize. The DEPS.md header still says '16 observed, 4 theorized' even though individual belief evidence lines were updated.
remediation_recommendations: Wire count verification into maintenance.py: lesson count, principle count, frontier count, domain count, belief evidence split should be auto-checked against filesystem state
Add staleness markers: numerical claims in FRONTIER.md should include the session when measured (e.g., 'meta 342L (S405)' not just 'meta 96L')
Header-body sync: DEPS.md interconnection model and INDEX.md frontier count should be updated whenever body content changes
Cross-file consistency check: K_avg, domain count, frontier count should have a single source of truth with other files referencing it

conclusion: Numerical claim drift has WORSENED since S388. Overall unsupported+contradicted rate is 35% (7/20), up from 20% at S387 and 5% at S388. The dominant failure mode is count drift: counts embedded in headers and cross-references are written once and never updated as the underlying data changes. Four of six contradictions are pure count staleness (frontiers, domains, theme sums, belief evidence split). The existence-claim robustness finding from prior retests is WEAKENED: K_avg has three conflicting values across three files, making even the 'existence' of a specific measurement value unclear. P-259 (existence-numerical claim asymmetry) remains directionally correct but the numerical failure rate is higher than the 5-20% range predicted.
